items.title: Migrated from java to python
Migrate test suite from Java to Python (pytest + requests)
items.body:
Full rewrite of a 100% Java/Maven/REST-Assured project into an idiomatic Python API test suite with equivalent coverage across all 11 endpoint groups (132 tests).

## Removed
- `pom.xml`, `src/test/java/` — entire Java build and test tree

## Added

### Core
- `requirements.txt` — `pytest`, `requests`, `python-dotenv`
- `spoonacular/client.py` — thin request wrapper (`get`, `post_json`, `post_form`, `post_raw`); reads `SPOONACULAR_API_KEY` from env, raises `EnvironmentError` with actionable message if missing
- `tests/conftest.py` — session-scoped `api_key` fixture; calls `pytest.skip` (not `fail`) when key absent, mirroring the Java `Assumptions.assumeTrue` behaviour

### Tests (`tests/`)
One module per endpoint, positive + negative parity with original Java classes:

| Module | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| `test_complex_search.py` | `GET /recipes/complexSearch` |
| `test_recipe_info.py` | `GET /recipes/{id}/information` |
| `test_similar_recipes.py` | `GET /recipes/{id}/similar` |
| `test_ingredient_info.py` | `GET /food/ingredients/{id}/information` |
| `test_recipes_by_ingredients.py` | `GET /recipes/findByIngredients` |
| `test_analyze_recipe.py` | `POST /recipes/analyze` |
| `test_classify_cuisine.py` | `POST /recipes/cuisine` |
| `test_classify_product.py` | `POST /food/products/classify` |
| `test_map_ingredients.py` | `POST /food/ingredients/map` |
| `mealplanner/test_meal_planner.py` | Shopping-list POST + DELETE workflow |
| `mealplanner/test_delete_meal_planner.py` | DELETE edge cases (double-delete, bad hash, bad id format) |

### CI & config
- `.github/workflows/tests.yml` — Python 3.11, runs `pytest --tb=short -v` on push/PR; injects `SPOONACULAR_API_KEY` from repository secrets
- `.env.example` — committed key template; `.env` excluded via updated `.gitignore`
- `README.md` — rewritten: venv setup, `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `.env` config, `pytest` invocation

## Usage
```bash
cp .env.example .env        # fill in real key
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
pytest -v
```

- **Ollama added to `docker-compose.yml`** — no local Ollama install needed, runs as a container alongside Milvus

## How to run

```bash
# 1. Start all services (Milvus + Ollama)
podman-compose up -d

# 2. Pull the embedding model (first time only)
podman exec milvus-ollama ollama pull nomic-embed-text

# 3. Load data
cd python-loader
cp .env.example .env
pip install -r requirements.txt
python loader.py

# 4. Start the MCP server
cd ../mcp-server
cp .env.example .env
pip install -r requirements.txt
python server.py
```

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `MILVUS_COLLECTION` | `documents` | Collection name |
| `EMBEDDING_API_TYPE` | `ollama` | `ollama` or `openai` |
| `EMBEDDING_MODEL` | `nomic-embed-text` | Model name |

items.title: Fix user type parsing with/without default notation
items.body:
- Builds on earlier toString() work, and extends with always-sorted struct keys
- Adds example for windowed AVRO keys, including SensorKey schema
- Reverses the Python logic for single AVRO message generation and batch AVRO message generation
- Fixes user type parsing for `avro:windowed(SensorKey)` and `avro:windowed(string)` types
- Improves legibility of the UserTypeParser by splitting parsing logic into multiple methods

Additionally, to get the build and all tests working, the following is included in the PR:
- Removed the `@type` and `@schema` interpretations from `NativeDataObjectMapperWithSchema`  
- Example 11 was modified according to above change, so dynamic type or schema modification is not done (or allowed) anymore
- Split `ValuePrinter` into `JavaValuePrinter` and `PythonValuePrinter`, so `PythonDict` and `PythonList` can use the latter cleanly
- Repositioned code across `PythonDataObjectMapper` and `PythonTypeConverter` . The latter was replaced by `PythonNativeMapper` for name clarity (converts between Python and Java types) and is now also used by `PythonDataObjectMapper`.
- Added additional conversion logic for integer types <--> floating point types, and between `byte[]` and `List` types. This was necessary for proper passing of values to Python, and to allow some tests to succeed.
- Small improvements to `tuple` type handling in Java.
- Proxy objects for Python now all implement the `AbstractProxy` interface. When translating objects from Java to Python, this allows proper identification and ensures no additional conversion takes place.
- All proxy objects are moved into their own proxy package. This includes `LoggerBridge`, `MetricsBridge`, and related, and all store proxies.
- The objects `ValueAndTimestamp` and `VersionedRecord` are really not necessary in Python, since stores have alternative methods for putting values. When getting values, we now translate both object types into Python dictionaries. Because of this change, the `ValueAndTimestampFactory` is also no longer necessary. The factory test was also removed from `KSMLStateStoreTest`.

items.title: Port tdd-bank-account-java to Python 3.12+
items.body:
Python 3.12+ port of the XP Dojo TDD Bank Account kata, preserving the two-state (starter/solution) workshop structure from the original Java repo. ## Project structure ``` .github/workflows/ci.yml # GitHub Actions CI workflow src/bank/account.py # Starter: stubs raising NotImplementedError tests/test_account.py # Starter: 11 @pytest.mark.skip tests (red state) solution/src/bank/account.py # Solution: full implementation solution/tests/test_account.py # Solution: 19 passing tests solution/conftest.py # sys.path fix so solution tests resolve solution/src pyproject.toml # setuptools.build_meta + pytest config ``` ## Starter state All methods are stubs; tests are skipped. Participants remove one `skip` at a time and drive implementation via Red → Green → Refactor: ```python class Account: def deposit(self, amount: float) -> None: raise NotImplementedError # ... ``` ```python @pytest.mark.skip(reason="not yet implemented") def test_deposit_increases_balance(self) -> None: account = Account() account.deposit(100) assert account.balance() == 100 ``` ## Solution state Full implementation covering all six user requirements — deposit, withdraw, transfer (with insufficient-funds guard), balance slip with timestamp, and filterable statement: ```python def transfer(self, amount: float, target: Account) -> None: self.withdraw(amount) # raises ValueError if insufficient funds target.deposit(amount) def statement(self, filter_type: Optional[TransactionType] = None) -> str: ... # header + per-transaction rows + running balance footer ``` ## CI A GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) runs on push and pull_request to all branches: - **`main` branch**: the test step is skipped — the starter code only contains `@pytest.mark.skip` tests and the `solution/` directory does not exist there. - **Feature / PR branches**: runs `pytest -v` (11 skipped starter tests) followed by `pytest solution/tests -v` (19 passing solution tests). ## Other - `README.md` updated with setup instructions, project layout table, and pytest command reference. - `.gitignore` added for `__pycache__`, `.pytest_cache`, and build artifacts.
Original prompt > Act as a Senior Software Engineer specializing in Python and TDD. I want you to port the Java-based 'tdd-bank-account-java' repository to a modern Python 3.12+ equivalent. > > **Source Context:** > - Repository: https://github.com/xp-dojo/tdd-bank-account-java > - Branch 1 (Master): Contains the initial project structure, failing tests, or "starter" code. > - Branch 2 (instructors_walkthrough): Contains the completed, refactored solution with passing tests. > > **Requirements:** > 1. **Two-State Architecture:** Provide the code in two distinct sections: > - **The Starter (Master equivalent):** A clean project structure with the necessary classes and test skeletons (failing tests). > - **The Solution (Walkthrough equivalent):** The fully implemented logic with passing tests and refactored code. > > 2. **Modern Python Standards:** > - Use `pytest` for the testing framework (or standard `unittest` if you prefer zero external dependencies). > - Use Type Hinting (e.g., `from typing import ...`). > - Keep dependencies minimal (Standard Library preferred). > - Follow PEP 8 style guidelines. > > 3. **Documentation:** > - Update `README.md` to reflect the Python environment (setup instructions, how to run tests using `pytest`). > - Ensure the README mentions the `slides.pptx` as a supporting resource for the workshop. > > 4. **Project Structure:** > - Organize it logically (e.g., `src/` for logic, `tests/` for tests). > > Please generate the file content for the README, the starter tests/logic, and the final solution tests/logic.

items.title: Complete Java-to-Python testcontainers conversion with full module parity
items.body:
Converts all testcontainers-java modules and features to Python, achieving 100% feature parity.

## Implementation

**Core Infrastructure**
- GenericContainer with complete Java API surface
- Ryuk/ResourceReaper for automatic cleanup (socket-based protocol)
- DockerClientFactory, network management, Docker Compose support
- All 7 wait strategies (healthcheck, log, port, HTTP/TLS, shell, exec, composite)

**Modules Converted (58 total)**
- 24 databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra, Neo4j, InfluxDB, CouchDB, Oracle (Free/XE), MS SQL, ClickHouse, CockroachDB, Couchbase, CrateDB, DB2, Memcached, Milvus, OceanBase, QuestDB, YugabyteDB, Databend, Pinecone
- 5 messaging: Kafka, RabbitMQ, ActiveMQ, Pulsar, NATS
- 7 search/vector: Elasticsearch, Solr, ChromaDB, Qdrant, Weaviate, Typesense, Redpanda
- 17 cloud/services: LocalStack, MinIO, Vault, NGINX, Selenium, MockServer, Toxiproxy, Azure, GCloud, Consul, LDAP, Grafana, K3s, K6, HiveMQ, ScyllaDB, Ollama
- 4 data processing: Presto, Trino, TiDB, OrientDB
- 1 JDBC base class

## Conversion Approach

Python implementations match Java sources exactly:
- Docker images, tags, and exposed ports from Java `DockerImageName` and `addExposedPort()`
- Environment variables and commands from Java `addEnv()` and `setCommand()`
- Wait strategies and initialization logic from Java container classes
- Fluent API preserved with snake_case method names

Example conversion (PostgreSQL):

```python
# Java: new PostgreSQLContainer<>("postgres:13")
# Python:
from testcontainers.postgres import PostgresContainer

with PostgresContainer("postgres:13") as postgres:
    connection_url = postgres.get_connection_url()
```

## Quality

- 510+ tests (100% pass rate)
- Full PEP 484 type hints
- Zero security vulnerabilities (CodeQL verified)
- Pytest fixtures for all modules

See `PROJECT_STATUS.md` for complete feature comparison and module inventory.

items.title: Refactor: Migrate entire codebase from Java/Spring Boot to Python
items.body:
## Summary

Complete migration of the PreTrade multi-agent trading system from a Java/Spring Boot architecture to a pure Python implementation. All three agents (Market Analyst, Trade Executor, Learning Summary) and the Trade Dashboard web app are now implemented in Python using Flask, with shared utilities and database models refactored accordingly.

## Key Changes

**Removed (Java/Spring Boot):**
- All Maven modules: `agent-market-analyst/`, `agent-trade-executor/`, `agent-learning-summary/`, `trade-dashboard/`, `shared-db/`, `shared-utils/`
- Spring Boot application classes, controllers, services, and repositories
- JPA entity models (StockAnalysis, TradeDecision, PaperTrade, DailySummary, etc.)
- Spring configuration files (application.yml, pom.xml files)
- Java utility classes (NseClient, TimeUtils, Formatters, LotSizes)
- Comprehensive test suites across all modules
- Local setup documentation (`py/LOCAL_SETUP.md`)
- Individual agent Dockerfiles and `py/run.sh` script

**Added (Python):**
- **Shared modules** (`shared/`):
  - `nse_client.py` — HTTP client for NSE API calls with session warmup and retry logic
  - `time_utils.py` — IST time utilities for market hours and expiry calculations
  - `formatters.py` — Indian financial formatting (INR, percentages, PnL)
  - `lot_sizes.py` — NSE F&O lot size registry and validation
  - `models.py` — SQLAlchemy ORM models (replaces JPA entities)
  - `database.py` — Database connection and session management

- **Agent implementations** (Flask apps):
  - `market_analyst/app.py` — Market Analyst agent (port 8081)
    - `collectors/nse_collector.py` — NSE pre-market data, option chains, market snapshots
    - `collectors/news_collector.py` — Google News RSS and MoneyControl scraping
    - `collectors/technical_collector.py` — Volume profiles and VWAP calculations
    - `csv_export.py` — CSV export functionality
  - `trade_executor/app.py` — Trade Executor agent (port 8082)
  - `trade_dashboard/app.py` — Trade Dashboard web app (port 8080)
    - `csv_parser.py` — CSV signal parsing
    - `static/css/dashboard.css` — Dashboard styling
    - `templates/` — HTML templates (dashboard, upload, base)
  - `learning_summary/app.py` — Learning Summary agent (port 8083)

- **Test suite** (`tests/`):
  - `test_lot_sizes.py`, `test_time_utils.py`, `test_formatters.py`
  - `test_csv_parser.py`, `test_technical_collector.py`

- **Docker & Build**:
  - Individual Dockerfiles for each agent (`Dockerfile.analyst`, `Dockerfile.dashboard`, `Dockerfile.executor`, `Dockerfile.learner`)
  - Updated `docker-compose.yml` to reference new Dockerfiles
  - Simplified `scripts/build.sh` and `scripts/run.sh` for Python workflow
  - Updated `scripts/test.sh` for pytest

items.title: 4-step mushroom identification pipeline: new design, Java/Python backends, and robustness fixes
items.body:
Introduces a new end-to-end 4-step mushroom identification flow across the Flutter client and backend services, including backend auto-detection (Java proxy vs Python FastAPI), a new Step 2 tree-traversal UI, and new Python/Java pipeline modules to support Steps 1–4.

## Changes Made

### Flutter client
- Refactors the identification flow into explicit Steps 1–4 with a new `TreeTraversalPage` route
- Updates `ResultsPage` to accept and normalise Step 4 output alongside the legacy `/identify` response
- **Constructor injection** for `IdentificationApiService` in `IdentificationProvider` to support test mocking
- **Safe JSON casts**: `visible_traits`, `final_recommendation`, and `confidence_breakdown` now use `Map<String, dynamic>.from()` to avoid `TypeError` crashes on `Map<dynamic, dynamic>` decoded responses
- **Derived Step 2 progress counts**: `step2AutoAnswers`/`step2UserAnswers` are now derived from `auto_answered.length` / `path.length` with fallback to explicit numeric fields, so the progress UI and auto-answered chip update correctly
- **`mounted` guards**: `_confirmAndContinue` in `CameraPage` now checks `mounted` before calling `setState` after `await`, preventing "setState() called after dispose()" errors

### Python backend
- Adds pipeline modules: visual trait extraction, `key.xml` tree traversal, trait DB comparison, final aggregation
- Exposes new FastAPI endpoints for Steps 2–4
- **`key.xml` encoding fix**: converted from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8 so Swedish characters (å/ä/ö) display correctly and string matching in `key_tree_traversal.py` works reliably; parser updated to use `encoding="utf-8"`

### Java backend
- New Spring Boot service that proxies the Python API under `/api/*`
- **Image content-type fix**: proxy now forwards the original `Content-Type` from the uploaded file (with `APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM` fallback) instead of hardcoding `IMAGE_JPEG`

### Makefile
- `PROJECT_ROOT` and `FLUTTER_BIN` are now overrideable (`?=`) and default to `$(CURDIR)` / `flutter` from PATH, removing hardcoded developer-specific paths that would break CI and other contributors

### Tests
- Added pipeline unit tests for `IdentificationProvider` using a `_FakeApiService` stub injected via the new constructor, covering `_applyStep2Response` (question shape, conclusion shape, derived counts, explicit-field precedence) and `runStep3AndStep4` sequencing (success, step-3-error stops step-4, missing prerequisite early exit)

## Summary
Converted the `awake` script from Jython (Python running on JVM) to native Java, eliminating the Jython dependency while maintaining full feature parity.

## Key Changes
- **Language migration**: Rewrote entire script from Jython to Java 11
- **Shebang update**: Changed from Jython execution to direct Java source execution using `--source 11` flag
- **Argument parsing**: Replaced argparse library with manual switch-case argument parsing
- **Time handling**: Converted from Python's `time` module to Java's `System.currentTimeMillis()` with proper millisecond-to-second conversions
- **Signal handling**: Replaced Python's signal handler with Java's `Runtime.addShutdownHook()` for graceful shutdown
- **Duration formatting**: Implemented custom `formatDuration()` method to replace Python's `timedelta` formatting
- **Class structure**: Organized code into a single `Awake` class with static methods
- **Build artifacts**: Removed `.jython_cache` from `.gitignore` as it's no longer needed

## Notable Implementation Details
- Maintains the same command-line interface with `-H`, `-M`, `-S`, and `-s` flags
- Preserves the 10-second check interval and 500ms sleep between iterations
- Uses `Point.equals()` for location comparison instead of Python's equality operator
- Properly handles time calculations in milliseconds while maintaining second-based duration logic
- Includes help text output matching the original argparse format

items.title: fix: Fix issue with _read_plane_tiled by ensuring tile_data is copied from Java buffer to Python
items.body: If you try to load data that exceeds MAX_JAVA_ARRAY_SIZE, the tiling code path is used. It was returning the same buffer multiple times, e.g.: The test for this was passing because the image was small, so only a single tile was sufficient. By using monkeypatch to reduce MAX_JAVA_ARRAY_SIZE and force multiple tiles the test fails. With the help of Copilot (Codex-5.2) and Talley, identified the buffer fetching as the source of the issue. The tile_data needs to be copied from the Java to Python side. With that fix, the test passes and doing something like loading resolution=0 of CMU-1.svs in napari returns the correct image.

items.title: Refactor: Replace Java AI service with Python FastAPI + patch vulnerable dependencies
items.body:
Replaces the Spring Boot AI service with a Python/FastAPI service using LangChain + ChromaDB + Ollama (Mistral 7B), and simplifies the architecture to three active services. Also patches five CVEs in `requirements.txt`.

## Architecture

```
Client → API Gateway :8080 → /api/ai/** (StripPrefix=1) → AI Service :8086
                                                            ↑
                                              Discovery Service :8761 (Eureka)
                                                            ↓
                                              Ollama :11434 (Mistral 7B)
```

## New `ai-service` (Python/FastAPI)

- **RAG pipeline** — LangChain + ChromaDB with `paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2` embeddings; auto-indexes knowledge base on first start
- **LLM** — Ollama/Mistral 7B with graceful OpenAI GPT-3.5 fallback; degraded-mode message when neither is available
- **Endpoints** — `POST /ai/chat`, `POST /ai/translate` (FR/EN/AR/ES), `POST /ai/analyze-document`, `GET /ai/health` + `/actuator/health`
- **Persistence** — async SQLite (aiosqlite + SQLAlchemy) for conversation history
- **Discovery** — registers with Eureka via `py-eureka-client` at startup
- **Knowledge base** — 6 administrative procedures (CPAM, CAF, titre de séjour, impôts, banque, université) + 10 FAQ entries as JSON, vectorised into ChromaDB

## `api-gateway` changes

Added explicit route in `application.yml`; Eureka URL now reads from `EUREKA_SERVER_URL` env var:

```yaml
routes:
  - id: ai-service
    uri: lb://AI-SERVICE
    predicates:
      - Path=/api/ai/**
    filters:
      - StripPrefix=1   # /api/ai/chat → /ai/chat
```

## Infrastructure

- **`docker-compose.yml`** — stripped to `discovery-service`, `api-gateway`, `ai-service`, `ollama`; vector store on a named volume (`/data/vectorstore`)
- **`pom.xml`** — removed `user-service`, `demarches-service`, `document-service`, `analytics-service`, `ai-service` (Java) modules
- **`start-services.sh`** — rewritten; `ollama pull mistral` runs in the background to avoid blocking startup
- Disabled Java services retain their directories with a `README` noting their status

## Security fixes (`requirements.txt`)

| Package | Before | After | CVE |
|---|---|---|---|
| `langchain-community` | 0.2.0 | 0.3.27 | XXE injection · pickle deserialization |
| `langchain` | 0.2.0 | 0.3.27 | ecosystem alignment |
| `langchain-openai` | 0.1.7 | 0.2.14 | ecosystem alignment |
| `python-jose` | 3.3.0 | 3.4.0 | ECDSA algorithm confusion |
| `python-multipart` | 0.0.9 | 0.0.22 | arbitrary file write · DoS |

## 🎯 Objectif

Refactoriser complètement le projet pour ne garder que **3 services** :

1. **`discovery-service`** (Spring Boot / Eureka) — inchangé
2. **`api-gateway`** (Spring Boot / Spring Cloud Gateway) — adapté pour router vers le nouveau AI service Python
3. **`ai-service`** (🆕 **Python / FastAPI**) — complètement réécrit en Python

Supprimer (ou vider/ignorer) tous les autres services : `user-service`, `demarches-service`, `document-service`, `analytics-service`.

---

## 📐 Architecture cible

```
Client (Next.js / curl)
        │
        ▼

│   API Gateway    │  :8080  (Spring Cloud Gateway)
│  (Spring Boot)   │

   ┌─────┴──────┐
```

---

## 📋 Travail détaillé à réaliser

### 1. Nettoyer le projet racine

- Supprimer les dossiers `user-service/`, `demarches-service/`, `document-service/`, `analytics-service/` (les retirer du `pom.xml` parent aussi)

### 2. Réécrire `ai-service` en Python (FastAPI)

```
ai-service/

```

#### Détails techniques clés :

**`app/main.py`** :
- Créer l'application FastAPI avec titre "IA-DAF AI Service"
- Au démarrage (`startup` event) :

  - S'enregistrer auprès d'Eureka via `py-eureka-client` avec app name `AI-SERVICE`, port 8086, health URL `/ai/health`

items.title: Migrate from Java/Swing to Python 3 with Flask web interface
items.body:
Complete rewrite from Java (Gradle/Swing) to Python 3 with dual interface support: Flask web application and Tkinter fallback. Maintains mathematical parity with original 2008 implementation.

## Changes

**Removed**
- All Java sources (`src/main/java/`, `src/test/java/`)
- Gradle build system and wrapper
- ~2,300 lines

**Added** 
- Core fractal algorithms in Python (`src_python/fractal_chaotica/`)
  - `mandelbrot.py`, `julia.py`, `biomorph.py`, `lambda_fractal.py`, `cubic_mandelbrot.py`
  - Uses native `complex` type, eliminating custom `ComplexNumber` class
- Flask web server (`web_app.py`) with RESTful image generation API
- Tkinter desktop GUI (`gui.py`, `main.py`) 
- Interactive HTML5 frontend (`templates/index.html`) with canvas-based zoom
- 11 unit tests verifying algorithm parity
- ~1,029 lines

**Security**
- Disabled Flask debug mode by default (controlled via `FLASK_DEBUG` env var)

## Usage

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python3 src_python/web_app.py  # Web interface at localhost:5000
python3 src_python/main.py     # Desktop GUI
```

## Breaking Changes
- Requires Python 3.8+ instead of Java 11+
- Gradle build no longer supported

## Known Limitations
- Pure Python rendering (no NumPy vectorization) - slower for large images
- No input validation on web API parameters
- Unpinned dependencies in requirements.txt

items.title: feat: Python/Polars GTFS Validator Migration (SpecOps Rewrite)
items.body:
# Python/Polars GTFS Validator Migration

## Overview

This PR introduces a complete rewrite of the GTFS validator from Java to modern Python using Polars for tabular data processing. This follows the **SpecOps methodology** for systematic migration and validation.

## SpecOps Migration Approach

This rewrite follows the SpecOps methodology which emphasizes:

1. **Behavioral Equivalence** - The Python validator produces output equivalent to the Java validator (`report.json`, `system_errors.json`)
2. **Clean Architecture** - Orchestrator owns I/O, parsing, schema enforcement; validators own pure domain logic
3. **Incremental Validation** - Each validator is tested against real feeds with diff analysis
4. **No Framework Parity** - We don't port Java framework patterns (reflection, DI containers, code generation)

## Key Changes

### Core Architecture
- `src/gtfs_validator/` - Python package with clean module separation
- `runner.py` - Orchestration pipeline
- `schemas.py` - Schema registry for GTFS tables
- `loading.py` - Polars-based data loading
- `validators/` - Cross-table business validations

### Validators Migrated
See `specops/validator-list.md` for complete migration status.

### Documentation
- `specops/llm-validator-authoring.md` - Template for LLM-assisted validator authoring
- `specops/validator-migration-prompt.md` - Migration prompt template
- Updated `AGENTS.md` with Python development guidelines

## Stack

- Python 3.12+
- Polars for tabular data
- `uv` for dependency management
- `pytest` for testing
- `mypy`/`pyright` for type checking
- `ruff` for linting/formatting

## Testing

```bash
uv sync
uv run pytest -q
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy src
```

## Notes

This is a **rewrite**, not a line-by-line port. The Python implementation:
- Uses idiomatic Python patterns
- Leverages Polars for vectorized operations
- Maintains strict separation of concerns
- Preserves behavioral equivalence with Java output

---

**Review focus**: Architecture correctness, validator behavior parity, test coverage.

items.title: fix(edges): add qualified names and language filtering to prevent cro…
items.body:
add qualified names and language filtering to prevent cross-language misresolution

Adds dst_qualified_name and src_language columns to pending_edges (schema v3) so that import edges carry the full import path and source language. Updates lookupSymbolIDTx to prefer same-language matches and reject cross-language candidates, preventing a Java import from resolving to a Python symbol with the same short name. All 6 language import handlers now extract qualified paths (e.g. "java.util.List", "net/http", "std::collections::HashMap").

Closes #16

Additionally, this PR fixes a pre-existing failing test in `src/concurrency/thread_local.rs` which was blocking the `cargo test` suite from succeeding.

Add language-specific linting and formatting for Python and Java bots
items.body:
The repo's mixed Python/Java/JS codebase caused ESLint and Prettier to run against non-JS files, producing spurious failures. This adds per-language lint/format checks with proper scoping.

## New workflow: `language-specific-lint.yml`

Four-job pipeline triggered on push/PR to `main`/`master`:

- **`detect-languages`** — diffs changed files, outputs `has_python` / `has_java` / `has_js` flags to gate downstream jobs
- **`lint-python`** — Flake8 (120-char limit) + Black format check, scoped to `python_bots/`
- **`lint-java`** — Checkstyle + google-java-format, scoped to `java_bots/`; JARs cached between runs
- **`lint-js`** — ESLint + Prettier restricted to `.js/.ts/.jsx/.tsx` only; skipped when no JS/TS files changed

Each job runs only when its language category has changed files, and each carries `permissions: contents: read`.

## Supporting files

- **`checkstyle.xml`** — Google Java Style–based rules (naming, braces, imports, whitespace)
- **`python_bots/README.md`** / **`java_bots/README.md`** — directory conventions and local tool usage
- **`.prettierignore`** — adds `*.java`, `python_bots/`, `java_bots/` to prevent Prettier from touching non-JS files

## Summary

Verified **all Python examples** in the cookbook against a live CUBRID 11.2 Docker instance. Found and fixed 3 issues.

## Bugs Fixed

### 1. `quickstart/5min-sqlalchemy/app.py` — Wrong database name
- **Before**: `DATABASE_URL = "cubrid+pycubrid://dba@localhost:33000/demodb"`
- **After**: `DATABASE_URL = "cubrid+pycubrid://dba@localhost:33000/testdb"`

items.body:
## Summary

Batch of CI improvements to the consolidated `ci.yaml`:

- **Remove `codeflash-java-runtime/` from unit_tests detection** — Java runtime changes are validated by `e2e-java`, not unit tests
- **Narrow `e2e` flag** from `codeflash/` to explicit Python subdirs — excludes `languages/java/` and `languages/javascript/` which have their own E2E jobs
- **Narrow `tests/` in `e2e_java`/`e2e_js`** to `tests/scripts/end_to_end_test_java*` / `..._js*` — avoids triggering language E2Es on unrelated test changes
- **Extract Validate PR into composite action** (`.github/actions/validate-pr/action.yml`) — single source of truth, was duplicated 3× across E2E jobs
- **`fetch-depth: 1` for unit-tests and type-check** — these jobs don't need git history, saves checkout time
- **Remove `continue-on-error: true` from unit-tests** — was silently masking real failures in the matrix
- **`git add -u` instead of `git add -A` in prek** — only stages modified tracked files, won't accidentally commit untracked files
- **Remove `name: ${{ matrix.name }}`** from matrix jobs — prevents literal `matrix.name` in GitHub UI when jobs are skipped
- **Remove JDK 11 + Maven from unit-tests** — only 5/3943 tests reference Java, all have `skip_if_maven_not_available()` guards

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify workflow-only PR skips all jobs correctly (gate reports "skipped")
- [ ] Verify code PR triggers appropriate jobs based on changed paths
- [ ] Verify composite validate-pr action works on E2E jobs

items.body:
### Summary

Introduce an initial evaluation suite for feature identification. The suite runs four LLM-generated datasets:
- Obvious evidence: explicit version strings / clear signals
- Inference required: indirect signals (ecosystem/library cues) to validate inference tagging
- Ambiguous evidence: weak or conflicting hints to ensure the model remains conservative and avoids over-identifying
- False positives: misleading mentions in user content

Evaluation currently uses an LLM-as-a-judge approach via criteria. There are known limitations (synthetic data, subjectivity..) but this initial evaluation is intended as a baseline and a specification of expected behavior that should provide a good starting point.

### Testing

- Start scout server

```
node scripts/scout.js start-server --stateful
```

- Run evaluations

```
EVALUATION_CONNECTOR_ID=<evaluator-llm> node scripts/playwright test \
  --config x-pack/platform/packages/shared/kbn-evals-suite-streams/playwright.config.ts \
  --project <identification-llm> \
  --grep "features identification"
  ```

```java
public static List<Integer> fibonacci(int n) {
    if (n <= 0) return new ArrayList<>();
    if (n == 1) { ... }
    List<Integer> fib = new ArrayList<>(List.of(0, 1));
    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++)
        fib.add(fib.get(i - 1) + fib.get(i - 2));
    return fib;
}
```

items.title: feat: report generation performance optimizations (convert once + msgpack)
items.body:
## Performance Optimizations for Report Generation (Issue #255)

This PR implements several performance optimizations for the MeetManager-Tools report generation system, significantly reducing the time required for large meet bundles on Cloud Run.

### Changes:
1. **"Convert Once" Architecture**: 
   - Refactored `backend/src/server.py` to perform the expensive `MmToJsonConverter.convert()` (which handles table joins and data normalization) only ONCE per bundle request in the main process.
2. **Msgpack Binary Serialization**:
   - Replaced heavy IPC pickling of large dictionaries with `msgpack` binary serialization to `/tmp` (tmpfs on Cloud Run). Workers now read the pre-converted data directly from the filesystem, reducing IPC overhead.
3. **Algorithmic Optimizations**:
   - Optimized `add_entries_to_event` in `backend/src/mm_to_json/mm_to_json.py` using `pandas.groupby` to replace O(N) table scans with O(1) lookups.
   - Refactored `_read_table_jackcess` to optimize the Java-to-Python row conversion during initial MDB loading.
4. **Decoupled Reporting**:
   - Updated `ReportDataExtractor` to accept pre-converted data, allowing workers to bypass the heavy conversion logic.

### Verification:
- **Benchmarks**: Sequential generation of 5 Meet Programs decreased from ~73.5s to ~61.3s (~17% improvement) on a 4MB dataset. Improvement is expected to be even more significant for larger meets (20MB+) due to reduced IPC overhead and O(1) lookups.
- **Unit Tests**: All 68 backend tests pass, including fixes for fixture path handling in Docker and lazy-loading of grouped tables.
- **Manual Verification**: Verified `msgpack` serialization/deserialization speed (~30ms for 4MB).

Closes #255

items.title: Add JSON serialization, WIRE_SIZE constants, and equals/hashCode to Java/Python
items.body:
## Summary

This PR implements four major feature additions to the Java and Python backends to improve parity with the C++ reference implementation:

1. **JSON/Dict Serialization**: Add `toMap()`/`fromMap()` methods to Java classes and `to_dict()`/`from_dict()` methods to Python classes for easy serialization to/from dictionary/map representations.

2. **WIRE_SIZE Constants**: Generate `WIRE_SIZE` static constants for all fixed-size structs, messages, and bitmap classes in both backends.

3. **equals() and hashCode()**: Implement proper equality and hashing for Java classes (including special handling for byte arrays and numeric primitives).

4. **String Representations**: Add `__hash__()` and `__repr__()` methods to Python types and improve `toString()` for Java Flags/Scaled types.

## Key Changes

### Java Backend (`java_backend.cpp`)
- **BitReader/BitWriter optimization**: Add fast path for byte-aligned reads/writes (when both bit position and count are byte-aligned)
- **Serialization**: Generate `toMap()` and `fromMap()` methods for both regular and bitmap classes with proper type handling for enums, scaled types, structs, byte arrays, and lists
- **Equality**: Implement `equals()` and `hashCode()` with special cases for byte arrays and primitive numeric types
- **WIRE_SIZE**: Emit `WIRE_SIZE` constant when available from analyzer
- **Enum improvements**: Support long-typed enum values for >32-bit enums (previously truncated to int)
- **String representations**: Add `toString()` for Flags (hex format), Scaled (value format), and other types

### Python Backend (`python_backend.cpp`)
- **BitReader/BitWriter optimization**: Add fast path for byte-aligned reads/writes using `int.from_bytes()` and `to_bytes()`
- **Serialization**: Generate `to_dict()` and `from_dict()` methods for both regular and bitmap classes with proper handling of nested objects, enums, scaled types, and bytes
- **Hashing and repr**: Add `__hash__()` and `__repr__()` methods to all type classes (Flags, Scaled, etc.)
- **WIRE_SIZE**: Emit `WIRE_SIZE` constant when available from analyzer
- **Auto-count fix**: Handle optional arrays in auto-count encode (check for None before calling len())
- **Exception handling**: Narrow exception catching in `decode_frame` from bare `Exception` to specific `(DecodeError, ConstraintError)`

### Documentation (`limitations.md`)
- Update feature parity table to reflect all fixes from four audit rounds
- Move resolved features from "Missing Features" to "Resolved Features" section
- Update severity assessment: all MEDIUM-severity bugs now fixed
- Note that Java and Python backends now have near-complete feature parity with C++

## Implementation Details

- **Serialization design**: Java uses `java.util.Map<String, Object>` while Python uses native `dict` for consistency with each language's idioms
- **Type conversions**: Both backends handle conversion of complex types (enums, scaled values, nested structs) during serialization/deserialization
- **Byte array handling**: Special logic for byte arrays in equality checks (using `Arrays.equals()` in Java) and serialization (converting to/from lists of integers)
- **Optimization**: Byte-aligned bit operations now use direct byte copying instead of bit-by-bit manipulation, improving performance for aligned data
- **Enum bit width**: Java enums now use `long` for values when bit width > 32, preventing truncation

items.title: Migrate from Java to Python Triplifier implementation with real-time progress tracking
items.body:
## Python Triplifier Migration - Complete with Progress Tracking ✅

Successfully migrated Flyover from using the legacy Java-based Triplifier application to the new Python Triplifier package from PyPI, including real-time progress tracking for enhanced user experience.

### ✅ **Migration Complete:**

This PR replaces the Java-based Triplifier implementation with a modern Python-based solution that provides:

1. **PyPI Package Integration** - Uses official `triplifier>=2.2.0` package
2. **Direct API Calls** - Imports and calls Python functions directly (no subprocess overhead)
3. **Real-Time Progress Tracking** - Shows actual conversion progress (percentage and rows processed) instead of generic loading animation
4. **Environment Variable Support** - Configurable database settings
5. **File Path Compatibility** - Correct output locations for upload workflow
6. **Dependency Optimization** - Removed Java runtime, added necessary tools (curl)

### 🔧 **Key Changes:**

**Progress Tracking System:**
- **Progress Tracker Module**: Thread-safe `ProgressTracker` class in `utils/progress_tracker.py`
- **API Endpoint**: New `/api/triplifier-progress` REST endpoint for progress queries
- **UI Integration**: Enhanced submit button shows percentage, rows processed, and current stage
- **Long-Running Support**: Designed to handle processes that can take multiple hours for large datasets

**Triplifier Integration:**
```python
# Before (Java subprocess)
command = f"java {java_opts} -jar triplifier.jar -p {properties_file}"
output = gevent.subprocess.check_output(command, shell=True, ...)

# After (Direct Python API with progress tracking)
from pythonTool.main_app import run_triplifier
progress_tracker.update_progress(task_id, status='triplifying', percentage=50)
args = Args()  # Configure parameters
run_triplifier(args)  # Direct function call
```

**Configuration:**
- Replaced Java `.properties` files with dynamic YAML configuration
- Environment variables: `TRIPLIFIER_DB_URL`, `TRIPLIFIER_DB_USER`, `TRIPLIFIER_DB_PASSWORD`
- CSV data converted to SQLite for SQL-compatible processing

**Docker Optimization:**
- Removed Java Runtime Environment (JRE)
- Removed JDBC driver downloads
- Removed Java environment variables
- Added `curl` for upload functionality
- Smaller container images
- Faster builds

**Code Organization:**
- Created `utils/python_triplifier_integration.py` for all triplifier functions
- Created `utils/progress_tracker.py` for progress tracking system
- Helper methods for database preparation and configuration
- Proper file path handling (`/app/ontology.owl`, `/app/output.ttl`)

### ✅ **Bug Fixes:**

**Triplifier Package Bug Workaround** - COLUMNREFERENCE not defined
- **Issue**: The triplifier package v2.2.0 has a bug where calling data generation separately triggers a `NameError`
- **Solution**: Using combined mode (`ontologyAndOrData=None`) which generates both files in a single call, avoiding the bug
- **Status**: Modular functions (`generate_ontology_csv`, `generate_data_csv`, etc.) are available but documented as having this known limitation

### ✅ **Testing & Verification:**

- ✅ Docker build succeeds with Python-only dependencies
- ✅ Direct API function calls working in container
- ✅ YAML configuration generation works correctly
- ✅ File paths match upload workflow expectations
- ✅ curl available for GraphDB uploads
- ✅ RDF-store successfully populated
- ✅ Progress tracking provides real-time updates
- ✅ Backward compatibility maintained

### 📋 **Benefits:**

- **Clean Dependencies**: Official PyPI package instead of JAR files
- **Direct Integration**: Same-process execution, no subprocess overhead
- **Better User Experience**: Real-time progress visibility for long-running processes
- **Enhanced Debugging**: Direct Python stack traces and error handling
- **Row-Level Tracking**: Shows exact number of rows processed with percentage completion
- **Stage Awareness**: Different messages for each processing stage (preparing, loading, triplifying)
- **Flexibility**: Environment-based configuration
- **Maintainability**: Single-language Python codebase
- **Performance**: Smaller images, faster builds, reduced container size
- **Official Support**: Uses maintained PyPI distribution

### 📊 **Progress Tracking Example:**

Users now see detailed progress instead of generic loading:
1. "Preparing database..." (0%)
2. "Loading data... 33% (50,000 / 150,000 rows)"
3. "Loading data... 67% (100,000 / 150,000 rows)"
4. "Triplifying data... 100% (150,000 / 150,000 rows)"
5. "Completed!"

The migration preserves all existing functionality while modernizing the implementation for better maintainability, integration, and user experience.

items.title: Part 2: Web-based binary-to-source previewer
items.body:
## What
A browser-based tool that reads any native binary and shows what the program would look like in high-level languages.

## Features
- **Drag & drop** or file picker to load binaries
- **5 language tabs**: Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go
- **Mach-O ARM64 decoder**: Parses MOVZ, ADR, SVC instructions to detect syscalls
- **ELF support**: Basic detection with string-based fallback
- **Syntax highlighting** with copy-to-clipboard
- **Zero dependencies**, no build step — just open `index.html`

## Files
```
previewer/
├── index.html          # main page with drop zone + tabbed UI
├── style.css           # dark theme styling
├── app.js              # binary parser + tab controller
└── languages/          # per-language code generators
    ├── java.js
    ├── javascript.js
    ├── python.js
    ├── ruby.js
    └── go.js
```

## How to test
```bash
open previewer/index.html
# Drop the 'hello' binary onto the page
# Switch tabs to see Java, JS, Python, Ruby, Go equivalents
```

## Screenshot flow
1. Open page → see drop zone
2. Drop `hello` binary → detects Mach-O ARM64
3. Parses ARM64 instructions →
4. Switch tabs → see `System.out.print`, `console.log`, `puts`, `print()`, `fmt.Println`

items.title: Implement structured LLM cost tracking and UI reporting
items.body:
This PR introduces structured LLM usage cost tracking and reporting across the system. 

Key changes include:
- **Core API**: Added an optional `Double cost` parameter to `IConsoleIO.showNotification` to allow passing numeric values alongside human-readable messages.
- **Backend**: Updated `HeadlessHttpConsole` to include this structured cost in `NOTIFICATION` events sent to the UI.
- **Python App**: Implemented cost accumulators in the `BrokkApp` to track `current_job_cost` (resetting per turn) and `session_total_cost`.
- **UI Enhancements**: 
    - The `StatusLine` now displays the current turn and total session costs in USD.
    - The `TokenBar` includes the session cost in its usage tooltip/label.
- **Testing**: Added comprehensive unit tests for cost tracking logic in both Java and Python.

These changes provide users with real-time visibility into the financial consumption of their LLM interactions.

Fixes #2844

items.title: CORE-3228: Add raw_probability to session proto
items.body:
## Summary
- Add `double raw_probability` field to `SessionCreated` message (field 4) — combined probability of all selections in the session
- Add `double raw_probability` field to `SessionMarketOutcome` message (field 3) — per-outcome probability before margin

## Context
Client Betclic (ID: 260) requested probabilities on OBB session selections to support cashout calculations ([OGC-1486](https://oddingg.atlassian.net/browse/OGC-1486)). Saibot already calculates these probabilities internally; this change exposes them in the session response proto so Fujin can surface them to clients.

## Notes
- Proto-only change — generated code (Go/Java/JS/Python) needs to be regenerated via `generate.sh`
- Fully backward compatible — new `double` fields default to `0.0` for existing clients
- Fujin implementation tracked in [CORE-3228](https://oddingg.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-3228)

items.title: Add Java translation of hello.py
items.body:
Converts `hello.py` to idiomatic Java as required, placing the output in `java_translation/Hello.java`.

## Changes

- **`java_translation/Hello.java`** — New file. Translates three functions and the main entry point:
  - `greet(String name)` → string concatenation
  - `add(int a, int b)` → integer addition
  - `fibonacci(int n)` → iterative `List<Integer>` build, matching Python semantics (empty list for `n≤0`, `[0]` for `n=1`)

```java
public static List<Integer> fibonacci(int n) {
    if (n <= 0) return new ArrayList<>();
    if (n == 1) { ... return List([0]); }
    List<Integer> fib = new ArrayList<>(List.of(0, 1));
    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++)
        fib.add(fib.get(i - 1) + fib.get(i - 2));
    return fib;
}
```

Original `hello.py` is untouched.
